---
title: Geo Data Cleaning
toc: true
reference-location: margin
fig-cap-location: bottom
appendix-style: default
citation-location: document
citation:
  container-title: Switchbox
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
---

# BEFORE RUNNING THIS NOTEBOOK, RUN THE FOLLOWING :

# 1. run just fetch-all-data # 2. clean_peoples_construction_polygons.py # 3.

match_parcels_buildings.py

# Imports

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```

# Constants

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```

# Utility Functions

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```

# Data Loading

Load all geospatial datasets for analysis.

## Peoples Gas Construction Polygons

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```

## City Blocks

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```

## Cook County Parcels with Units (Pre-processed)

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```

## Chicago Street Centerlines

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```

# Spatial Clipping

Clip all datasets to only include features that fall within Peoples Gas construction polygons.

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```

## Blocks in Construction Areas

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```

## Parcels in Construction Areas

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```

# Assign Parcels to Blocks

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```

## Streets in Construction Areas

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```

## Count Parcels and Units at block level

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```

## Exlucde blocks with 2 or less parcels

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```

## Calculate Street Miles

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```

# Write out intermediate clipped datasets

Export the clipped datasets before aggregation to block or polygon level.

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```

## Combined Summary Dataset

At this point we have: - summary statistics at the unclippedblock level for parcel and residential unit counts - summary
statistics at the clipped block level for street miles. these contain duplicate records for blocks that overlap multiple
construction polygons.

We need to aggregate the street miles by geoid10 to get the total street miles for each block and merge into a final
dataset where each row is one block that contain counts of all parcels (within 8m of a construction polygon) and the
apportioned street miles.

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```

# Export Final Dataset

Export the block-level summary statistics with full block geometries to GeoJSON.

```{python}
27 files left unchanged
```
